questions.text: If the unit vectors i, j, k are perpendicular to each other therefore;i.i = j.j = k.k = _.I.j = j.k = k.i = _.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the dot product of a unit vector with itself is always 1, not 0.
This is the correct answer because the dot product of a unit vector with itself (e.g., i.i) is 1. The dot product of two different perpendicular unit vectors (e.g., i.j) is 0.
This option is incorrect because the dot product of a unit vector with itself is not 0; it is 1.
This option is incorrect because the dot product of two different perpendicular unit vectors is 0, not 1.

questions.explanation: The dot product of two vectors A and B is given by A.B = |A||B| cosθ. If the angle θ between the vectors is 0°, cosθ = 1, so a vector dotted with itself (i.i, j.j, k.k) is 1, due to the unit magnitude of the vectors. If θ is 90°, cosθ = 0, so the dot product of two perpendicular vectors (i.j, j.k, k.i) is 0. Therefore, the correct answer is 1 for i.i = j.j = k.k, and 0 for i.j = j.k = k.i. The other options are incorrect as they do not accurately reflect these properties of the dot product for unit vectors.

questions.text: A particle moves from position r= 3i+2j-6k to r= 14i+13j+9k under the action of a Force F= 8i+2j+6k. Find the work done by the force:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect according to the explanation given below.
This option is incorrect according to the explanation given below.
This option is incorrect according to the explanation given below.
This option is incorrect according to the explanation given below.

According to the calculation given below, this is the correct answer. Total Work Done= work done in x + work done in y + work done in z Since, Work done= Force x distance moved in the direction of forceDistance moved from r1 to r2 is= r2 – r1 = (14i+13j+9k) - (3i+2j-6k) = (11i+11j+15k)Force= 8i+2j+6kHence, Work done (x)= force (x) x Distance moved in x-axis = 8 x 11 = 88 Work done (y)= force(y) x Distance moved in y-axis = 2 x 11 = 22 Work done (z)= force (y) x Distance moved in z-axis = 6 x 15 = 90Total work done= 88 + 22 + 90=200

questions.explanation: Work done = force×displacement Given data:F=8i+2j+6k N Now displacement, d=(14i+13j+9k) — (3i+2j-6k)d=(14–3)i+(13–2)j+(9-[-6])k d=11i+11j+15k Now, W=F×d W= (8i+2j+6k) × (11i+11j+15k) W=(11×8)+(2×11)+(6×15)W=88+22+90 W=200J

questions.text: A vector such as the velocity of a body undergoing uniform translational motion, which can be displaced parallel to itself and applied to any point known as:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: A vector whose magnitude is one is called a unit vector.

A free vector is one whose action is not confined or associated with a unique line in space. For a body in uniform translational motion, the velocity of any point in the body may be taken as a vector and this vector will equally well describe the velocity of all points in the body.
A directionless vector whose magnitude is zero is called a null vector.
A position vector is defined as a vector that symbolizes either the position or the location of any given point concerning any arbitrary reference point.
A resultant vector is defined as a single vector that produces the same effect as is produced by a number of vectors collectively.

questions.explanation: Option B is the correct answer because a free vector is one whose action is not confined or associated with a unique line in space. For a body in uniform translational motion, the velocity of any point in the body may be taken as a vector and this vector will equally well describe the velocity of all points in the body. Hence, the velocity of such a body may be represented by a free vector.

questions.text: All of the following is/are scalar quantities except:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Temperature is a scalar quantity. It is characterized only by its magnitude, indicating the degree of hotness or coldness, and does not involve any direction.
Density is a scalar quantity, defined as mass per unit volume. It is expressed by a numerical value without direction.
Volume is a scalar quantity that measures the three-dimensional space occupied by a substance or object. It is described solely by its magnitude.
Force is a vector quantity. It is defined by both magnitude and direction, representing a push or pull acting upon an object.
Speed is a scalar quantity that refers to how fast an object is moving. It has magnitude but no direction.

questions.explanation: Scalar quantities are defined by having only magnitude, such as temperature, density, volume, and speed. These quantities do not involve any direction.Force is the exception here as it is a vector quantity, meaning it has both magnitude and direction, which is why it is not a scalar quantity.While temperature, density, volume, and speed are described without direction, force is characterized by the direction in which it acts as well as its strength.

questions.text: R1 and R2 are two-position vectors making angles θ1 and θ2 with positive X-axis respectively. Their vector product is:[R1 = 4 cm, R2 = 3 cm , θ1 = 30 degrees , θ2 = 90 ]
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. It results from a miscalculation of the sine component or swapping of angles.
This is the correct answer. The vector product is calculated using the formula |R1 × R2| = |R1||R2|sin(θ2 - θ1). Substituting the given values, we find |R1 × R2| = 6√3 cm, directed along the Z-axis.
This option is incorrect due to an error in simplifying the trigonometric component or incorrect angle substitution.
This option is incorrect. It likely stems from an incorrect use of the trigonometric function or angle values.
This option is incorrect and results from either a calculation error in the sine component or incorrect angle usage.

questions.explanation: The vector product of two vectors, R1 and R2, is given by the formula |R1 × R2| = |R1| |R2| sin(θ2 - θ1), where θ is the angle between the vectors. Here, |R1| = 4 cm, |R2| = 3 cm, θ1 = 30°, and θ2 = 90°. Thus, |R1 × R2| = 4 × 3 × sin(90° - 30°) = 4 × 3 × 1/2√3 = 6√3 cm.This calculation confirms that the correct answer is 6√3 cm, directed along the Z-axis.The incorrect options result from errors in either the trigonometric calculations or angle substitutions, leading to incorrect values.

questions.text: If A = 3i + 6j - 2k, then the unit vector parallel to A will be:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because it does not correctly calculate the magnitude of vector A.
This option is correct as it divides each component of vector A by its magnitude, 7, resulting in the unit vector.
This option incorrectly uses 14 as the magnitude, which is incorrect for vector A.
This option represents vector A itself, not the unit vector parallel to A.
This option multiplies by the magnitude instead of dividing, which is incorrect for finding a unit vector.

questions.explanation: To find a unit vector parallel to a given vector A = 3i + 6j - 2k, first calculate the magnitude of A: |A| = √(3² + 6² + (-2)²) = √(9 + 36 + 4) = √49 = 7. The unit vector, denoted as û, is then calculated by dividing each component of A by its magnitude: û = (1/7)(3i + 6j - 2k) = (3/7)i + (6/7)j - (2/7)k. Therefore, the correct answer is (1/7) * (3i + 6j - 2k). The other options either use incorrect magnitudes or represent the vector A itself, not the unit vector.

questions.text: What is the projection of E = 2i-3j+6k onto the direction of vector F=i+2j+2k?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is incorrect because the calculations for the dot product and magnitude do not result in this value. Recheck the dot product and magnitude calculations.
This is correct. By calculating the dot product of E and F and dividing it by the magnitude of F, we find that the projection is indeed 8/3.
This is incorrect because the calculations for the dot product and magnitude do not result in this value. Recheck the dot product and magnitude calculations.
This is incorrect because the calculations for the dot product and magnitude do not result in this value. Recheck the dot product and magnitude calculations.
This is incorrect because the calculations for the dot product and magnitude do not result in this value. Recheck the dot product and magnitude calculations.

questions.explanation: The explanation is as follows:To find the projection of vector E onto the direction ofvector F, we can use the formula: Projection of E onto F = (E dot F) / |F| First, let's calculate the dot product of E and F: E dot F =(2)(1) + (-3)(2) + (6)(2) = 2 - 6 + 12 = 8 Next, we need to find the magnitude of vector F: |F| = √(1² + 2² + 2²) = √9 = 3 Now we can calculate the projection: Projection of E onto F= (E dot F) / |F| = 8 / 3 ≈ 2.67 Therefore, the projection of vector E onto the direction ofvector F is approximately 2.67.

questions.explanation: The scalar product of two vectors is computed as the product of their magnitudes and the cosine of the angle between them. In this case, vector k is perpendicular to both i and j, meaning the angle between them is 90 degrees. The cosine of 90 degrees is zero, hence the scalar product is zero. That's why option A is correct. All other options are incorrect as they do not account for the perpendicular nature of the vectors, leading to a scalar product of zero.

questions.text: The resultant of two forces has magnitude of 20N. If one of the forces is of magnitude 20√3 N and makes an angle of 30o with the resultant then, the other forces must be of magnitude:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Please refer to the main explanation text.

To calculate the magnitude of the other force, we can use trigonometry and vector addition. The given force is 20√3 N at an angle of 30 degrees. Let's break down the force into x and y components:The x-component of the force = 20√3 * cos(30°) = 20NThe y-component of the force = 20√3 * sin(30°) = 10NSince the resultant force is 20N, and one force is already 20N, the other force should balance out the y-component, which is 10N. Therefore, the magnitude of the other force must also be 20N to cancel out the y-component and give a resultant of 20N.

A vector in space has three dimensions, x, y and z.
A vector in space has three dimensions, x, y and z.

questions.explanation: The "three" dimensions are x, y, and z. Three-dimensional space (also: 3D space, 3-space or, rarely, tri-dimensional space) is a geometric setting in which three values (called parameters) are required to determine the position of an element (i.e., point). This is the informal meaning of the term dimension.

questions.text: A body will be in translational equilibrium if the vector sum of all the forces acting on it is:

The net force of the body must be zero for the body in equilibrium.
The net force of the body must be zero for the body in equilibrium.

questions.explanation: The vector sum of all of the forces acting on a body is a single force called the net force. If the net force is equal to zero, the object is said to be in equilibrium. The law of equilibrium states that the sum of all forces/torques on a body has to be "Zero" for the body to be considered in equilibrium based on it the body is moving or not, the equilibrium is classified as static or dynamic.

questions.text: The vector product of two vectors A and B is _ vectors A and B.
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Not equal to the product of magnitudes of
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. The vector product, or cross product, results in a vector, not a scalar, and its magnitude is given by |A||B|sin(θ), not just the product of magnitudes.
This option is incorrect. The cross product results in a vector that is perpendicular to, not parallel to, the plane containing the original vectors.
Correct! The cross product of two vectors A and B results in a vector that is perpendicular to the plane containing A and B, as determined by the right-hand rule.

options.text: Less in magnitude than the product of magnitudes of
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. The magnitude of the cross product is based on |A||B|sin(θ), and it can be less than, equal to, or greater than the simple product of magnitudes, depending on the angle between the vectors.

questions.explanation: The vector product, or cross product, of two vectors A and B results in a vector perpendicular to the plane containing A and B. This is determined using the right-hand rule, which helps visualize the direction of the resulting vector. The magnitude of the cross product is |A × B| = |A||B|sin(θ), where θ is the angle between A and B. Option C correctly identifies the perpendicular nature of the resulting vector. Option A is incorrect because it misrepresents the nature of the cross product as a scalar. Option B is incorrect because it misinterprets the orientation as parallel rather than perpendicular. Option D is incorrect because it inaccurately describes the magnitude relationship without considering the sine component.

questions.text: Displacement of sun with respect to earth is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Correct. At any given instant, the displacement of the Sun with respect to Earth is approximately r, which is the radius of Earth's nearly circular orbit. Displacement is the shortest path between two points.
Incorrect. 2πr is the circumference of the Earth's orbit, representing the total path length of one full revolution, not the displacement at any specific moment.

Incorrect. 2r might represent the displacement if the Earth moved directly across its orbit from one side to the opposite side, but this is not generally the case for displacement at any given moment.
Incorrect. r² represents the square of the radius, which is not related to displacement. Displacement is a vector quantity that involves distance and direction, not squared values.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is r. Displacement is a vector quantity that describes the shortest path between two points, taking into account direction. At any given moment, the displacement of the Sun with respect to the Earth is approximately the radius of Earth's nearly circular orbit, r. The other options are incorrect because they do not accurately describe displacement: 2πr is the path length of a full orbit, 2r is only applicable in a specific situation not generally true at any moment, and r² is unrelated to displacement.

questions.text: Instantaneous velocity is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect according to the explanation given below. Instantaneous velocity is the velocity of an object at a specific instant in time. It is the rate of change of the object's displacement with respect to time at that precise moment. In other words, it is the slope of the object's position-time graph at a particular time point.Mathematically, the instantaneous velocity (v) of an object at time t can be expressed as the derivative of its displacement (s) with respect to time (t):v(t) = ∆s/∆twhere:v(t) = Instantaneous velocity at time t∆s = Infinitesimal change in displacement (distance traveled) during an infinitesimally short time intervaldt = Infinitesimal change in time during that same infinitesimally short time intervalIf we want to know the instantaneous velocity at a specific moment t₀, we take the limit of the average velocity over a shorter and shorter time interval Δt approaching zero:If we want to know the instantaneous velocity at a specific moment t₀, we take the limit of the average velocity over a shorter and shorter time interval Δt approaching zero:v(t₀) = lim(Δt → 0) Δs / ΔtAs the time interval Δt becomes infinitesimally small (Δt → 0), we get the instantaneous velocity at time t₀.In summary, instantaneous velocity gives us the exact velocity of an object at an exact point in time, taking into account any changes in speed and direction that may be occurring continuously during motion. It is a fundamental concept in calculus and physics, as it allows us to study the behavior of objects with precision as time changes.

This option is incorrect according to the explanation given below. Instantaneous velocity is the velocity of an object at a specific instant in time. It is the rate of change of the object's displacement with respect to time at that precise moment. In other words, it is the slope of the object's position-time graph at a particular time point.Mathematically, the instantaneous velocity (v) of an object at time t can be expressed as the derivative of its displacement (s) with respect to time (t):v(t) = ∆s/∆twhere:v(t) = Instantaneous velocity at time t∆s = Infinitesimal change in displacement (distance traveled) during an infinitesimally short time intervaldt = Infinitesimal change in time during that same infinitesimally short time intervalIf we want to know the instantaneous velocity at a specific moment t₀, we take the limit of the average velocity over a shorter and shorter time interval Δt approaching zero:If we want to know the instantaneous velocity at a specific moment t₀, we take the limit of the average velocity over a shorter and shorter time interval Δt approaching zero:v(t₀) = lim(Δt → 0) Δs / ΔtAs the time interval Δt becomes infinitesimally small (Δt → 0), we get the instantaneous velocity at time t₀.In summary, instantaneous velocity gives us the exact velocity of an object at an exact point in time, taking into account any changes in speed and direction that may be occurring continuously during motion. It is a fundamental concept in calculus and physics, as it allows us to study the behavior of objects with precision as time changes.

Instantaneous velocity is the velocity of an object at a specific instant in time. It is the rate of change of the object's displacement with respect to time at that precise moment. In other words, it is the slope of the object's position-time graph at a particular time point.Mathematically, the instantaneous velocity (v) of an object at time t can be expressed as the derivative of its displacement (s) with respect to time (t):v(t) = ∆s/∆twhere:v(t) = Instantaneous velocity at time t∆s = Infinitesimal change in displacement (distance traveled) during an infinitesimally short time intervaldt = Infinitesimal change in time during that same infinitesimally short time intervalIf we want to know the instantaneous velocity at a specific moment t₀, we take the limit of the average velocity over a shorter and shorter time interval Δt approaching zero:If we want to know the instantaneous velocity at a specific moment t₀, we take the limit of the average velocity over a shorter and shorter time interval Δt approaching zero:v(t₀) = lim(Δt → 0) Δs / ΔtAs the time interval Δt becomes infinitesimally small (Δt → 0), we get the instantaneous velocity at time t₀.In summary, instantaneous velocity gives us the exact velocity of an object at an exact point in time, taking into account any changes in speed and direction that may be occurring continuously during motion. It is a fundamental concept in calculus and physics, as it allows us to study the behavior of objects with precision as time changes.
This option is incorrect as the correct one is available.

In other words, it is the slope of the object's position-time graph at a particular time point.Mathematically, the instantaneous velocity (v) of an object at time t can be expressed as the derivative of its displacement (s) with respect to time (t):v(t) = ∆s/∆twhere:v(t) = Instantaneous velocity at time t∆s = Infinitesimal change in displacement (distance traveled) during an infinitesimally short time intervaldt = Infinitesimal change in time during that same infinitesimally short time intervalIf we want to know the instantaneous velocity at a specific moment t₀, we take the limit of the average velocity over a shorter and shorter time interval Δt approaching zero:If we want to know the instantaneous velocity at a specific moment t₀, we take the limit of the average velocity over a shorter and shorter time interval Δt approaching zero:v(t₀) = lim(Δt → 0) Δs / ΔtAs the time interval Δt becomes infinitesimally small (Δt → 0), we get the instantaneous velocity at time t₀.In summary, instantaneous velocity gives us the exact velocity of an object at an exact point in time, taking into account any changes in speed and direction that may be occurring continuously during motion. It is a fundamental concept in calculus and physics, as it allows us to study the behavior of objects with precision as time changes.

questions.text: If a rotating body is moving anti-clockwise, the direction of angular velocity is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is incorrect. Angular velocity is not directed towards the center; it is perpendicular to the plane of rotation.

This is incorrect. Angular velocity does not point away from the center; it is perpendicular to the plane of rotation.

options.text: Perpendicular to the plane of rotation, pointing towards you
options.explanation: This is correct. For a body rotating anti-clockwise, the right-hand rule indicates that the angular velocity vector points perpendicular to the plane of rotation, directed towards the observer.

questions.explanation: The direction of the angular velocity is along the axis of rotation. For an object rotating clockwise, the angular velocity points away from you along the axis of rotation. For an object rotating counterclockwise, the angular velocity points toward you along the axis of rotation.

questions.text: Consider a car is travelling for one hour. Which of the following trips have the same average velocity?
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Car travels 20 km due east and car travels 70 km due east
options.explanation: Option 1: * Car 1: 20 km east * Car 2: 70 km east * Net Displacement: Car 1 (20 km), Car 2 (70 km) - Different

options.text: Car travels 40 km due east, then turns around and travels 20 km due west and a car that travels 20 km due east
options.explanation: Option 2: * Car 1: 40 km east - 20 km west = 20 km east * Car 2: 20 km east * Net Displacement: Car 1 (20 km), Car 2 (20 km) - Same

options.text: Car travels 70 km due east and a car that travels 40 km due east, then turns around and travels 20 km due west
options.explanation: Option 3: * Car 1: 70 km east * Car 2: 40 km east - 20 km west = 20 km east * Net Displacement: Car 1 (70 km), Car 2 (20 km) - Different

options.text: Car travels 30 km due west, then turns around and travels 30 km due east and a car that travels 40 km due east, then turns around and travels 20 km due west
options.explanation: Option 4: * Car 1: 30 km west + 30 km east = 0 km * Car 2: 40 km east - 20 km west = 20 km east * Net Displacement: Car 1 (0 km), Car 2 (20 km) - Different

questions.explanation: Average velocity = total displacement/total timeWe know that displacement is a vector quantity. Therefore, let's assume that the west direction is negative and the east direction is positive throughout this question.For option B:Average velocity of the 1st car = {+40 + (-20)}/1 = 20 km/hrAverage velocity of the 2nd car = +20/1 = 20 km/hrThus B is the correct option.

questions.text: Two forces, 5 N and 10 N are acting at ‘O’ and ‘P’ respectively on a uniform rod of 100 cm is suspended at the position of center of gravity 50 cm mark as shown in the figure. What is position of P on meter rod?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: A) 80 cm: If P were at 80 cm, the distance from the pivot would be 80 - 50 = 30 cm (0.3 m). The clockwise torque would be 0.3 \text{ m} \times 10 \text{ N} = 3 \text{ Nm}. This would not balance the 2.5 Nm counter-clockwise torque.
For the rod to be balanced, the clockwise moment must be equal to the anticlockwise moment.5×50=10×xx=25cm 50+25=75cm.250/10=xAs it is a numerical, it can have only one possible answer.

B) 70 cm: If P were at 70 cm, the distance from the pivot would be 70 - 50 = 20 cm (0.2 m). The clockwise torque would be 0.2 \text{ m} \times 10 \text{ N} = 2 \text{ Nm}. This would not balance the 2.5 Nm counter-clockwise torque.

D) 65 cm: If P were at 65 cm, the distance from the pivot would be 65 - 50 = 15 cm (0.15 m). The clockwise torque would be 0.15 \text{ m} \times 10 \text{ N} = 1.5 \text{ Nm}. This would not balance the 2.5 Nm counter-clockwise torque.

questions.text: The vectors A and B are such that |A + B| = |A – B|, then the angle between the two vectors is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Cosine of 90° is zero, which does not apply in this scenario.
Incorrect, as the angle between the vectors is not 60° based on the given information.
Correct! By using the properties of vector magnitudes and the given equation, the angle between the vectors A and B is indeed 90°.
Not the correct answer. The angle between the vectors A and B is not 180° as per the equation provided.

questions.explanation: Consider the angle between A and B as ∅. The resultant of A + B vector is R = √(A²+B²+2AB cos∅) and the resultant of A - B vector is R = √(A²+B²-2AB cos∅). Given |A+B| = |A-B|, we can equate the expressions and solve for cos∅ to find ∅ = 90°. This is the correct angle between the vectors based on the provided equation.The other options are incorrect because they do not align with the properties of vector magnitudes and the equation given. Option A and D provide angles that are not supported by the equation, and Option B is based on a misinterpretation of the cosine function in this context.

questions.text: If A + B= 7i^ +7k^ and A-B= -i^ + k^, then the magnitude of A will be:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option does not match the calculated magnitude of A, which is 5. Option a is incorrect.
This option does not match the calculated magnitude of A, which is 5. Option b is incorrect.

Given: A + B = 7i^ + 7k^ A - B = -i^ + k^ To find the magnitude of A, we need to eliminate B from these equations. We can do this by adding the two equations together: (A + B) + (A - B) = (7i^ + 7k^) + (-i^ + k^) Simplifying the left side and right side separately: 2A = 6i^ + 8k^ Dividing both sides by 2: A = 3i^ + 4k^ Now we have the vector A. To find its magnitude, we can use the formula: |A| = √(Ax^2 + Ay^2 + Az^2) In this case, Ax = 3, Ay = 0, and Az = 4. Substituting these values into the formula: |A| = √(3^2 + 0^2 + 4^2) = √(9 + 0 + 16) = √25 = 5 Therefore, the magnitude of A is 5. Hence, option C is the correct answer.
This option does not match the calculated magnitude of A, which is 5. Option d is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Given:A + B = 7i^ + 7k^A - B = -i^ + k^To find the magnitude of A, we need to eliminate B from these equations. We can do this by adding the two equations together:(A + B) + (A - B) = (7i^ + 7k^) + (-i^ + k^)Simplifying the left side and right side separately:2A = 6i^ + 8k^Dividing both sides by 2:A = 3i^ + 4k^Now we have the vector A. To find its magnitude, we can use the formula:|A| = √(Ax^2 + Ay^2 + Az^2)Since i^,j^, and,k^ are the unit vectors for x,y, and z coordinate planes respectively.In this case, Ax = 3, Ay = 0, and Az = 4. Substituting these values into the formula:|A| = √(3^2 + 0^2 + 4^2)= √(9 + 0 + 16)= √25= 5Therefore, the magnitude of A is 5. Hence, option C is the correct answer.

questions.text: If two forces F1 and F2 act on a body and are balanced by a frictional force F3 so that the body is in equilibrium, then which of the following diagrams correctly represents the sum of vectors?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The three forces F₁, F₂, and F₃ are arranged tail-to-tail and pointing in different directions.There is no head-to-tail connection among the vectors.This does not represent vector addition or a closed triangle.So, it does not satisfy equilibrium condition.Conclusion: Incorrect

The vectors F₁ and F₂ are arranged head-to-tail.However, F₃ is also pointing outward rather than completing the triangle.The triangle does not close, and the resultant of F₁ + F₂ is not balanced by F₃.Again, this setup does not reflect zero net force.Conclusion: Incorrect

F₁ and F₂ are placed head-to-tail, but F₃ is pointing in the same general direction as the others.It fails to act as the opposing force to the resultant of F₁ + F₂.Therefore, it doesn’t complete the vector triangle properly.No equilibrium is achieved here either.Conclusion: Incorrect

This is the correct vector representation.F₁ and F₂ are placed head-to-tail, and then F₃ starts from the head of F₂ and ends at the tail of F₁, forming a closed triangle.This means:F₁ + F₂ + F₃ = 0, indicating the body is in perfect equilibrium.The frictional force F₃ is correctly shown as the balancing force against the resultant of F₁ and F₂.Conclusion: Correct

questions.explanation: This is the correct vector representation.F₁ and F₂ are placed head-to-tail, and then F₃ starts from the head of F₂ and ends at the tail of F₁, forming a closed triangle.This means:F₁ + F₂ + F₃ = 0, indicating the body is in perfect equilibrium.The frictional force F₃ is correctly shown as the balancing force against the resultant of F₁ and F₂.Conclusion: Correct

questions.text: Two forces equal in magnitude but opposite in direction and not acting along the same line constitute a couple
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Independent of the location of origin
options.explanation: This is the correct answer. A couple, consisting of two equal and opposite forces that do not act along the same line, produces a pure torque. The magnitude of this torque is independent of the location of the origin chosen to calculate it. This means that the rotational effect of a couple is the same regardless of where you measure it from.

options.text: Dependent on the location of origin
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The torque produced by a couple is independent of the location of the origin.
This is incorrect. A couple produces a net torque, not a net force. The net force of a couple is always zero.

This is correct. A couple always produces a net torque, which is a rotational force. The magnitude of this torque is equal to the product of one of the forces and the perpendicular distance between their lines of action.

questions.text: In three dimensional Cartesian Coordinate system (x,y,z), you have two vectors A and B. Vector A has components Ax= 4, Ay=3, and Az=2, while vector B has components Bx=1, By=5, and Bz=2. What is dot (scalar product) of vectors A and B?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: To find the dot product (scalar product) of vectors A and B, you multiply the corresponding components and add them together:A · B = Ax_Bx + Ay_By + Az_Bz= (4)_(1) + (3)_(5) + (2)_(2)= 4 + 15 + 4= 23So, the correct answer is b. 23.The dot product is a scalar value that represents the amount of "similarity" between the two vectors. In this case, the result is positive, indicating that the vectors have some degree of alignment.
The dot product is positive, not negative.
The vectors are not orthogonal (perpendicular), so the dot product is not zero.
This is not the correct result of the calculation.

questions.explanation: To find the dot product (scalar product) of vectors A and B, you multiply the corresponding components and add them together:A · B = Ax_Bx + Ay_By + Az_Bz= (4)_(1) + (3)_(5) + (2)_(2)= 4 + 15 + 4= 23So, the correct answer is b. 23.The dot product is a scalar value that represents the amount of "similarity" between the two vectors. In this case, the result is positive, indicating that the vectors have some degree of alignment.

questions.text: Law of inertia satisfies:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: For a body in equilibrium, it must satisfy two conditions. Firstly, the net force acting upon the object must be zero. Secondly, the net torque acting upon the object must also be zero. Newton’s first law of motion tells us that a body continues its state of rest or of uniform motion in a straight line if no resultant or net force acts on it. Therefore, the Law of inertia satisfies the conditions of equilibrium.
A force is said to be a variable force if magnitude or direction, or both change with time.
Contact force is a force that acts on objects when they are in physical contact with each other due to interaction.
The law of conservation of mass states that in a chemical reaction, mass is neither created nor destroyed.

questions.explanation: Law of inertia, also called Newton’s first law, states that an object will continue to be either in a state of motion or in a state of rest unless an external force acts on it.Diagram:

questions.text: A force of magnitude 10N is acting along the x-axis; its component along the y-axis is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is correct. Since the force is directed along the x-axis, there is no component along the y-axis. The y-component of a force that acts purely along the x-axis is always 0 N.
This is incorrect. A force acting solely along the x-axis cannot have a y-component unless it has a component in that direction.
This is incorrect. The force magnitude along the x-axis does not translate into the y-axis component in this scenario.
This is incorrect. While the force's total magnitude is 10N, it is entirely along the x-axis, yielding zero component along the y-axis.
This is incorrect. This value does not relate to the force's resolution into components.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Zero. When resolving forces into components, a force acting along the x-axis has no y-component because it is entirely in the horizontal direction. Mathematically, the y-component of a force F acting at an angle θ from the x-axis is calculated as Fy = F sin θ. Here, θ is 0 degrees since the force is along the x-axis, resulting in sin(0) = 0. Therefore, Fy = 10 N × 0 = 0 N. The other options suggest non-zero y-components, which are incorrect since they imply that the force has a component along the y-axis, which it does not.

questions.text: A set of coordinate axes in which measurements are made is called
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Cartesian coordinates define a 2D or 3D system using perpendicular axes. It is a specific type of coordinate system.This is only one specific type of a frame of reference.
Rectangular coordinates are synonymous with Cartesian coordinates. This is a specific type of frame of reference.This is only one specific type of a frame of reference.
Spherical coordinates use radius, azimuth, and polar angles. Used for 3D representation in spherical systems.This is only one specific type of a frame of reference.

A frame of reference is the broader concept that defines the system in which measurements are made. It consists of coordinate axes and a reference point, allowing observers to measure positions, velocities, and other physical quantities. Since the question asks about the system where measurements are made, this is the correct answer.

questions.explanation: A frame of reference is the broader concept that defines the system in which measurements are made. It consists of coordinate axes and a reference point, allowing observers to measure positions, velocities, and other physical quantities. Since the question asks about the system where measurements are made, this is the correct answer.

questions.text: Which of the following is a vector quantity?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Electric flux is a scalar quantity as it does not have a direction. It is calculated as the product of the electric field and the area through which the field lines pass, considering the angle between them.
Work done is a scalar quantity. It is defined as the product of force and displacement in the direction of the force, but it does not have a direction itself.
Electric potential energy is a scalar quantity. It refers to the energy stored due to the position of a charge within an electric field and lacks direction.
None of the listed quantities are vector quantities. A vector quantity is defined by both magnitude and direction, and none of the options provided meet this criterion.

questions.explanation: Vector quantities are those that have both magnitude and direction. In this question, none of the options provided are vector quantities. Electric flux, work done, and electric potential energy are all scalar quantities because they possess only magnitude without a directional component. Therefore, the correct answer is 'None of the given options'.
